key_insight: The paper reframes machine learning as a categorical factorization problem and defines non-compositionality as the failure of a sketch to factor through a quotient, then lifts this notion infinitesimally via tangent categories (LINCS).
engineering_relevance: If useful, this could provide a principled language for analyzing whether learning systems preserve compositional constraints under perturbations, which may matter for robust model design and theory.
method: Represent learning problems as sketches with commutativity, limit, and colimit constraints.
Define the base defect as a factorization obstruction and lift it through the tangent functor to get LINCS.
Iterate tangent lifting via the INC endofunctor to study stabilization as a coalgebraic fixed point.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports no completed empirical results; it says experimental evaluation is underway for deep learning, LLMs, and reinforcement learning in companion papers.
watch_item: The framework is highly abstract and depends on tangent-category assumptions plus a set-based class realization for final coalgebra existence, so practical implementation may be nontrivial.
